blocks.content: Zion tilted his head, recognizing the familiar face.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Surely... was it Vincent?
As they walked across the carpet, Arius explained.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's largely projected. The superego is heavily influenced by the father. Normally, it would be strong enough to suppress the Egoist.
Ignite greeted them with a broad smile.

blocks.speaker: Ignite
blocks.content: So, you say you have a cure for me?
Arius was momentarily speechless, feeling an overwhelming pressure as if facing a wild beast.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Unbelievable. Is this the weakened state of the superego?
In a normal person, the superego would be paralyzed in the face of death. But Ignite still exuded a powerful presence.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: This is truly impressive.
Even Arius, who had explored many minds, had never encountered such a strong superego.
A person with a criticism consciousness as solid as steel never lets their guard down. The rumor that Shirone could become a top-tier talent was based on this trait.
Zion snorted.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Is he bluffing even on the brink of death? What an annoying guy.
Arius thought that Zion's superego must be very weak.
There is no cure for my illness. Because this world is already sick. So, why have you come to see me? I'll overlook the first lie, but from now on, you must tell the truth.

blocks.speaker: Ignite
blocks.content: I am Ignite, the ruler of this city!
Ignite pointed his massive axe horizontally and shouted at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ignite
blocks.content: Shirone! Your selfishness is causing everyone to suffer. What do you, a commoner, dream of? Your power is not something you earned through your efforts! It should be given to others! Now, I will end all suffering with your death!
Amy looked at Arius with a bewildered expression.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Is that Shirone's critical consciousness? That's just typical loser logic.
Ignite lifted the massive axe resting against the chair with one hand.
As he grasped the weapon, the air grew as heavy as steel, pressing down on their chests.
Zion, terrified, drew his Armand.
Activating his golden armor, the sword split and encased him like armor.
At the same time, Ignite's subordinates raised their weapons.
Arius sighed in frustration.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Just hold them off for a moment. It will be over soon.
What are you going to do?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What are you going to do?
Arius approached Ignite, a bluish light of the mental magic Confuse gathering in his palm.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: ...I'll have to start mental reconstruction.
Cough! Cough!
Shirone's party coughed as they exited the door to the 7th stage.
The stench from the landfill was still imprinted in their minds, making it feel like the smell lingered.
But the situation here wasn't any better.
Shirone's party stood in front of the door, unable to move, with blank expressions.
The plaza floor was littered with corpses with burst heads.
People were falling from rooftops like rain.
Elsewhere, citizens were fighting each other to the death.
Even though it wasn't reality, it was the most horrific scene they had ever seen.
Armin watched the situation with a serious expression.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: If the actions of the projections were this extreme, it meant that Zenogor's influence was tightening faster than expected.
Shirone, watching his world crumble with sad eyes, suddenly wore a fierce expression.
He mischievously yanked Amy's hair.
Ouch!
Amy turned around with a bewildered look.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why did you do that?
Don't block my view. I can't see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't block my view. I can't see.
Amy pouted her lips.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She couldn't understand why Shirone, who was kind to everyone else, was only like this to her.
She wanted to hit him, but she had prepared herself before meeting the avatar, so she controlled her emotions.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Fine, sorry.
As Amy shrugged and turned away, Shirone became even more confused.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: She was a good person and on his side. So why did he feel bad?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why did you come to save me?
Amy flinched at the question from behind.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But without turning around, she thought for a moment and gave a short answer.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Because we're friends.
Shirone recalled a memory from reality.
It was a terrifying memory like a nightmare.
The world was upside down, and his body felt like it was burning.
He saw Amy cocooned on the opposite wall.
He couldn't guess the situation, but it was extremely unpleasant.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But you... didn't save me.
Amy lowered her head with a sad face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was it that? Did Shirone hate her for her weakness in not being able to act?
